meta.title: Zustand for Complex Form State
meta.excerpt: Patterns for managing multi-step form state with persistence, step tracking, and minimal boilerplate

props.body: React Hook Form handles field-level state excellently. But multi-step forms need more: step tracking, cross-step data sharing, and persistence. Zustand fills this gap with minimal complexity.

props.text: We evaluated several state management options. Zustand won because it provides exactly what multi-step forms need without the boilerplate of Redux or the limitations of Context.
Our form stores follow a consistent pattern: state sections map to form steps, with dedicated update methods for each section.
Zustand's persist middleware automatically syncs state to localStorage. Users can refresh the page or close the browser without losing their progress.

props.title: SSR Hydration
props.body: When using persist with Next.js, initial server render will not have localStorage data. This can cause hydration mismatches. Use conditional rendering or the onRehydrateStorage callback to handle this gracefully.

props.text: The isStepComplete function enables UI features like progress indicators and navigation guards that prevent skipping required steps.
Each form type has its own store with a unique localStorage key. Stores share a common interface so shared step components can work with any of them.

features.title: useFormStore
features.description: Service request form. 5 steps, stores under 'electrical-service-form' key.

features.title: useContactStore
features.description: Contact form. 5 steps, stores under 'electrical-contact-form' key.

features.title: useQuotationStore
features.description: Quotation form. 7 steps, stores under 'electrical-quotation-form' key.

props.text: Zustand automatically optimizes re-renders through selector subscriptions. Components only re-render when the specific state they use changes.

idealFor: Select multiple fields with shallow: useStore(s => [s.step, s.data], shallow)
Split into multiple small stores if unrelated state
notIdealFor: Subscribing to state you do not use
One massive store for entire app

props.body: Separate form field state (React Hook Form) from form flow state (Zustand). Use Zustand for step tracking, cross-step data, and persistence. Its minimal API, TypeScript support, and built-in persistence make it ideal for multi-step forms. Use selectors to optimize re-renders and keep stores focused on specific features.
